steps.title: Signal concession about accuracy
steps.content: The first sentence says the 1920 method seems rudimentary today. The next sentence pushes back by saying a modern study found almost the same center, proving the old method relatively accurate. The blank needs a concession signal.

steps.title: Reject wrong transition logic
steps.content: Choice A signals an example, but the second sentence does not illustrate rudimentariness. Choice C signals a result, though accuracy is not a result of seeming rudimentary. Choice D signals restatement, but the second sentence qualifies the first. The answer is B.
